title: Write Aiger Stack Overflow
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

The issue seems to happen when writing an aiger file for a design that has a wire loop in it.

I have attached two minimised files necessary for recreating the issue in a zip file:
+ design\_aiger.ys
+ design\_nomem.il

[bug.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/8826072/bug.zip)

To recreate, please run:
```
yosys -q design_aiger.ys
```

## Expected behavior

Wire loop should recognised when writing an aiger file, possibly emitting an error log, before gracefully stopping.

## Actual behavior

When run with the current master version of Yosys, this produces a segmentation fault.
```
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

Instrumenting Yosys with the Address Sanitizer confirms that this is a stack overflow occurring during the recursion of the bit2aig function.
Possibly the wire loop is preventing the recursion from terminating?

Address Sanitizer output:
```
ASAN:DEADLYSIGNAL
=================================================================
==27637==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: stack-overflow on address 0x7ffe9afe6eb0 (pc 0x5613a7e1f642 bp 0x7ffe9afe76b0 sp 0x7ffe9afe6e90 T0)
    #0 0x5613a7e1f641 in bit2aig backends/aiger/aiger.cc:71
    #1 0x5613a7e20eb2 in bit2aig backends/aiger/aiger.cc:88
    #2 0x5613a7e205f8 in bit2aig backends/aiger/aiger.cc:84
```
